bead.title: checks: residual production-reachability — internal/server providers and workers
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Current deadcode RTA on 2026-05-15 still reports residual server provider/worker helpers as unreachable from the production graph, so `ddx-83440482` cannot close from current-tree evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json` reports `cli/internal/server/providers.go:423` — `filterProviderOutcomes`, `providers.go:436` — `computeProviderPerformance`, and `workers.go:303` — `executeLoopIdleInterval`.

PROPOSED FIX
Resolve the symbols currently listed for these files in the committed artifact. WIRE them if the live server/provider path should still exercise them; DELETE them if the logic is dead or superseded. If deferred investigation is still needed after ~15 minutes, annotate the unresolved symbol with `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file the narrower follow-up before closing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch review-session or prompt residuals; those clusters are tracked separately.
- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.
bead.acceptance:
1. Every symbol currently listed for this cluster in `.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json` is either wired into the production graph or deleted.
2. `cd cli && go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./... | rg 'internal/server/(providers|workers)\.go'` returns no hits.
3. Any remaining `// wiring:pending` annotation created while resolving this cluster cites an open follow-up bead.
4. Decisions log written to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/decisions.md` records WIRE|DELETE|PENDING for each listed symbol.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
